prerequisites: A Miro Developer team (required to create apps in the Miro Developer Platform).
An app created in Miro under Profile settings > Your apps, with the required scopes (for example board:read) set in the app, and an access token obtained by installing the app to your Dev team.

authMethods.name: Access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single api_key secret, which is a Miro REST API access token. Miro issues this OAuth 2.0 access token when you install your app to a Dev team via the "Install app and get OAuth token" button, and it is sent as an Authorization: Bearer header on every API request. Note that by default Miro access tokens are long-lived unless you enable expiring tokens for the app, in which case they expire (60 minutes) and require an OAuth refresh flow that this single-secret connector does not perform.

setupSteps.title: Create a Miro app
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Miro, open Profile settings > Your apps, and select Create new app. Enter an app name. If you want the token to stay valid indefinitely for this connector, leave expiring tokens disabled (enabling them requires an OAuth refresh flow this connector does not implement).

setupSteps.title: Set the app scopes
setupSteps.body: In the app settings, add the scopes (permissions) your sync needs, for example board:read for read access to boards. Configure scopes via the app's permissions before installing it.

setupSteps.title: Install the app and copy the access token
setupSteps.body: On the app page, click "Install app and get OAuth token", choose your Dev team, and confirm with Install & authorize. Miro then displays the access token. Copy it.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied access token into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as Authorization: Bearer <token> when calling the Miro REST API.

sources.title: Quickstart - Build your first Miro REST API app
Miro authentication - Getting started with OAuth 2.0
